steps.title: Write the ticket-count equation
steps.content: $x$ is the number of 90-dollar tickets and $y$ is the number of 250-dollar tickets. Together they total 120 tickets: $$x + y = 120.$$

steps.title: Write the revenue equation
steps.content: The 90-dollar tickets bring in $90x$ dollars and the 250-dollar tickets bring in $250y$ dollars, for a total of 27,600 dollars: $$90x + 250y = 27,600.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A pairs $x + y = 120$ with $90x + 250y = 27,600$ — matches our system.
  • B uses $120 \times 27,600$ on the right, inflating the revenue total by a factor of 120.
  • C sets $x + y = 27,600$, which would mean 27,600 tickets sold, not 27,600 dollars.
  • D sets $90x = 250y$ (equal revenues) and $120x + 120y = 27,600$, neither of which captures 120 tickets sold for 27,600 dollars total.
That confirms A is correct.
